author: @guillermodelnoche

author: @kreynolds1123
text: Modify a coffee cup so heating a cup is more efficient. Then coat it to  make the inside food grade safe.

replies.author: @JavierChiappa
replies.text: Just paint the outside of the cup with the conductive ink and make a base to make contact.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text:
@JavierChiappa thay sure is simpler.  But, in doing that, you have to have heat go through the ceramic cup, and the whole cupbhas a larger surface area than just the bottom so maybe it could heat faster, but the parts your heating are on the outside of the cup doing a fair bit of warming air. 

It would be nice if we can just heat the inside of the cup and have the ceramic part of the cup insulate keeping  much of the heat inside.

replies.author: @ScarletFlames1
replies.text:
Have copper wire under foodgrade resin lead into a heating element at the bottom then. It's not magic and then all you'd need is a base to put the cup on that will touch connector connected to those wires.

If you want an actually good self heating cup you'd need to over engineer it because what's required of a cup and what's required of the heating elements is not very well aligned with each other. Best solution would be a heat conductive base connected to a heat transfer resistand side walls, heating element directly beneath the heat conducting base and then another heat resistant covering on the heating element + water proof connectors to power the thing from a dedicated stand.

replies.author: @JavierChiappa
replies.text: @ScarletFlames1 Man, if we are overenginnering the cup, you could use one of those stainless cups that have vacuum inside, paint the liquid wall inside with the ink and restore the vacuum inside the cup. So you would have, from inside out, liquid, stainless, ink, vacuum, stainless (outside wall)
